chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C. P. No. D–5877 of 2023 [Muhammad Saleem Vs. Muhammad Haneef Baloch & another] DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S). Present: Mr. Justice Muhammad Shafi Siddiqui,C.J. Mr. Justice Omar Sial Hearing 1. For orders on office objection. 2. For hearing of main case..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-. 11.09.2024 Mr. Aurangzeb, Advocate for the petitioner. Mr. M. Saad Siddiqui, Advocate for respondent No.2. Mr. Khaleeq Ahmed, D.A.G..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-. The petitioner has prayed that respondent No.1 be restrained from harassing the petitioner and character assassination and creating mental torture and further to restrain him from filing complaints in the office of the Anti-Corruption Department. 2. We have heard the petitioner’s counsel at some length and it seems that the petition on many counts is not maintainable. If the petitioner is being harassed, he has the forum available to raise grievances but under no stretch of imagination such accusations of the respondent No.1 could be adjudged to be correct or incorrect under Article 199 of the Constitution. The petitioner, if being aggrieved of such statement made by respondent No.1, he may exhausts the remedy available to him under the law before a competent forum having jurisdiction. The petition, since is not maintainable in view of the accusations and the prayer clauses, is dismissed leaving the petitioner at liberty to avail remedy available to him under the law. CHIEF JUSTICE JUDGE Asif
